notes:
Secret Wonders (DP3, 2007) is an early Diamond & Pearl-era set. DP-era booster = 10 cards: 5 common, 3 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo, 1 rare/rare-holo. Box = 36 packs, NO ETB (era predates ETBs). Available buckets: rare-holo:20, rare:24, uncommon:41, common:45, lv-x:2 (Gardevoir LV.X 131/132, Honchkrow LV.X 132/132). No secret rare in this set.

RARE SLOT (1/pack): Lv.X pull rate = 1:36 packs for the FIRST DP-era sets (Flipside Gaming + PokeBeach card-ratios), i.e. ~1 Lv.X per box. So lv-x = 1/36 ≈ 0.028. Remaining 0.972 split between rare-holo and rare using the set's own composition ratio (20 rare-holo : 24 rare): rare-holo = 0.972*(20/44) = 0.442, rare = 0.972*(24/44) = 0.530. (Sums to 1.0.)

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT (1/pack): any common/uncommon/rare/rare-holo can appear in reverse (Lv.X are not reverse-printed). Weighted by the reverse-printable card pool (common 45 + uncommon 41 + rare 24 + rare-holo 20 = 130): common 45/130=0.346, uncommon 41/130=0.315, rare 24/130=0.185, rare-holo 20/130=0.154. (Sums to 1.0.)

COMMON slot (5/pack) = 100% common. UNCOMMON slot (3/pack) = 100% uncommon.

CONFIDENCE = low: no per-set empirical pull study found; Lv.X rate is the published DP-era-average (1:36 for early sets) and the rare-holo/rare + reverse-slot splits are modeled from set composition, not a counted box break. Vintage sealed boxes carry a large collector premium, so EV/booster will be far below box price — expected, not a reason to inflate rates.
